Batavia Street Gang.description: New York independent street gang based in the Fourth Ward during the 1890s. Affiliated with the Eastman Gang, they were rivals of the Cherry Hill Gang. Known for a jewelry store robbery to fund costumes for a ball. Five members were arrested and tried in the incident.

descriptions: Batavia Street Gang were rivals of Cherry Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: they were rivals of the Cherry Hill Gang throughout the previous decade

descriptions: Batavia Street Gang was affiliated with Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliated with the Eastman Gang during the turn of the 20th century

descriptions: The Batavia Street Gang competed with the Cherry Hill Gang by hosting a party and robbing a jewelry store to outdo them.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the decade, rival gangs would attempt to compete with the gang's success. One incident in particular occurred when, after the Batavia Street Gang announced hosting a party at New Irving Hall, the Cherry Hillers planned to arrive in expensive wardrobe.

descriptions: Infobox lists Batavia Street Gang as an ally of Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang, Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: Listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Batavia Street Gang

Sicilian Mafia.description: The Sicilian Mafia, also known as Cosa Nostra, is an Italian criminal society originating in Sicily in the mid-19th century. It evolved from local protection and land control into a powerful criminal network involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The organization is structured into families or clans, each controlling a territory. It has influenced politics and finance and spawned diaspora groups like the American Mafia.

descriptions: The 'Ndrangheta is replacing the Sicilian Mafia as the dominant drug trafficker in North America.
edges.evidence_quote: Alex Perry's 2018 book... asserts that over the past decade, the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta has been supplanting the Sicilian Cosa Nostra as the dominant drug trafficking organization in North America.

descriptions: The two organizations cooperate in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrangheta and Sicilian Cosa Nostra groups are known to engage in joint ventures in cocaine trafficking operations.

descriptions: Barbaro 'ndrina cooperated with Sicilian Mafia in a cocaine trafficking cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: participating in a cartel of 'Ndrangheta families involved in cocaine trafficking with the Sicilian Mafia family of Mariano Agate

descriptions: The American Mafia cooperates with the Sicilian Mafia in criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Today, the Italian-American Mafia cooperates in various criminal activities with Italian organized crime groups, such as the Sicilian Mafia, the Camorra of Campania and the 'Ndrangheta of Calabria.

descriptions: American Mafia cooperates with Sicilian Mafia in criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Today, the Italian-American Mafia cooperates in various criminal activities with Italian organized crime groups, such as the Sicilian Mafia, the Camorra of Campania and the 'Ndrangheta of Calabria.

descriptions: American Mafia has links with Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The American Mafia, in addition to having links with organized crime groups in Italy such as the Camorra, the 'Ndrangheta, Sacra Corona Unita and Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: American Mafia and Sicilian Mafia collaborated in structuring the French Connection.
edges.evidence_quote: structured by mobster Lucky Luciano and the Italian-American mafia after their previous source... was shut down

descriptions: The American Mafia cooperates with the Sicilian Mafia in criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Hoy, la mafia estadounidense coopera en varias actividades criminales con los grupos de crimen organizado italianos, tales como la mafia siciliana, la Camorra de la Campania y la 'Ndrangheta de Calabria.

descriptions: The American Mafia originated as a branch of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: El nombre de la organización se deriva de la original Mafia o Cosa nostra , la mafia siciliana , con el término "mafia estadounidense" (o "mafia americana") haciendo referencia en un inicio sólo a los grupos de Sicilia que operaban en los Estados Unidos ya que esta organización se inició como una rama de la mafia siciliana formada por inmigrantes italianos en los Estados Unidos.

descriptions: The infobox lists Sicilian Mafia as an ally of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NAR, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra

descriptions: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia in heroin trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.

descriptions: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia to import heroin.
edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.

descriptions: Bonanno family had close ties and cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia to import heroin.
edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.

descriptions: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia to import heroin into the US.
edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.

descriptions: A Bonanno faction allied with a Sicilian faction for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Pistone testified that he was told that a Bonanno faction headed by Dominick Napolitano had formed an alliance with a Sicilian faction, which involved Salvatore Catalano.

descriptions: The Buffalo family was linked to Sicilian Mafia families in international drug-trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The investigation concluded with 200 arrested that linked the Sicilian Mafia families (Spatola, Gambino and Inzerillo) and the American Mafia families in Buffalo, New York City, Philadelphia, Chicago and San Francisco with international drug-trafficking.

descriptions: Camera di Controllo is modeled after the Sicilian Mafia Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: The body, also referred to as the Commission in reference to its Sicilian counterpart

descriptions: The Camorra and Sicilian Mafia jointly operated a money laundering scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1993, the Camorra and the Sicilian Mafia were accused of running a $1.5 billion operation to launder drug money through banks, insurance companies, hotels and casinos in Italy and on the French Riviera, arresting 36 suspects.

descriptions: The Castellammare del Golfo family is a part of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Francesco Domingo (born 1956) is a member of the Sicilian Mafia and the boss of the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia is listed as an ally of AGC in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel , CJNG , Brothers Keepers , 'Ndrangheta , Sicilian Mafia , Los Choneros , Los Contrabandistas , Los Cachiros , Los Rastrojos

descriptions: The Corleonesi waged a campaign to dominate the Sicilian Mafia (Cosa Nostra).
edges.evidence_quote: He initiated a campaign to dominate Cosa Nostra and its narcotics trade.

descriptions: The Detroit Partnership cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia to distribute heroin.
edges.evidence_quote: Utilizing its close links with the Sicilian Mafia, the Partnership became a major distributor of heroin in North America after World War II.

descriptions: The Gambinos and Sicilian Mafia were forming an alliance for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Operation Old Bridge broke up a growing alliance between the Gambinos and the Sicilian Mafia, which wanted to get further into the drug trade.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
descriptions: The infobox lists Sicilian Mafia as an ally of Brise de Mer.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hornec family , American Mafia , Sicilian Mafia , 'Ndrangheta , Clan del Golfo , FLNC

descriptions: Hard Livings cooperated with Sicilian Mafia in diamond and cannabis trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Internationally they have been known to have cooperated with the Sicilian mafia in the trafficking of illegal diamonds as well as trafficking cannabis to Europe.

descriptions: Los Zetas and Sicilian Mafia are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tijuana Cartel , Juárez Cartel , 'Ndrangheta , Los Mazatlecos , Barrio Azteca , MS-13 , Texas Syndicate , Triad , The Office of Envigado , La Línea , Hezbollah , Sicilian Mafia , Los Zetas Group Bravo

descriptions: NCO was hostile to the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: It was distinctly hostile to the Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia favored the Nuova Famiglia in the conflict with NCO.
edges.evidence_quote: the Sicilian Mafia to accommodate an agreement between the two warring clans, favouring the Nuova Famiglia

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia was affiliated with the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: affiliated with the Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: Infobox lists Sicilian Mafia as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: The Polverino clan supplied hashish to the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Polverino, the leader of the Polverino clan before his arrest, was considered the 'hashish king', due to his monopoly on the importation of hashish from Morocco to Italy via Spain supplying not only all the Camorra clans, but also the 'Ndrangheta, the Sicilian Mafia and the Sacra Corona Unita.

descriptions: Polverino clan supplied hashish to the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Polverino, was dubbed the 'hashish king', due to his monopoly on the importation of hashish from Morocco to Italy via Spain supplying not only all the Camorra clans, but also the 'Ndrangheta, the Sicilian Mafia and the Sacra Corona Unita.

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Family is a constituent family of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: He was the boss of the Santa Maria di Gesù Family in Palermo.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and 'Ndrangheta have had very close ties, with mutual affiliations between bosses.
edges.evidence_quote: Il rapporto con la 'Ndrangheta è stato molto stretto tanto che capibastone di spicco come Antonio Macrì, Giuseppe Piromalli, Mico Tripodo (compare d'anello di Totò Riina) si affiliarono a Cosa nostra e viceversa, capi della mafia siciliana si affiliano alle 'ndrine.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta participated in Cosa Nostra's bombing campaign in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2017 l'operazione 'Ndrangheta stragista rivelerebbe un primo coinvolgimento della 'ndrangheta nelle operazioni stragiste degli anni '90 di Cosa nostra.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra buys drugs from 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal XXI secolo si registra un declino dell'organizzazione siciliana rispetto a quella di origine campana e calabrese, il procuratore di Catanzaro Nicola Gratteri ha più volte affermato questa tendenza, tanto da dichiarare, supportato dalle numerose operazioni anti-ndrangheta, che ora Cosa nostra messinese si rifornisce dalle 'ndrine per l'acquisto di droga

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and 'Ndrangheta cooperate in drug trafficking and waste management.
edges.evidence_quote: A novembre 2017 il neoprocuratore nazionale antimafia Federico Cafiero De Raho conferma il rapporto tra le due organizzazioni criminali non solo nel campo degli stupefacenti, ma anche nell'ambito del settore dei rifiuti e della bonifica

descriptions: Cosa Nostra member relied on 'Ndrangheta for drug imports.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2025, nell'ambito dell'operazione del Comando provinciale dei Carabinieri di Palermo ... è emerso che Nunzio Serio, reggente del mandamento di Tommaso Natale, faceva riferimento ad Emanuele Cosentino, esponente della 'ndrina Gallico di Palmi, per importare grandi quantitativi di cocaina e hashish dalla Calabria in Sicilia.

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia helped end the Second 'Ndrangheta war and advised creating the Camera di Controllo.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sicilian Mafia contributed to the end of the conflict and probably suggested the subsequent set up of a superordinate body, similar to the Sicilian Mafia Commission

descriptions: Cosa Nostra yielded the illegal drug trade to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1990s, the weakened Cosa Nostra had to yield most of the illegal drug trade to the 'Ndrangheta crime organization from Calabria.

descriptions: The Arena 'Ndrangheta clan had direct contact with Sicilian mafia bosses.
edges.evidence_quote: Le indagini dei Carabinieri hanno rivelato che Nicola Arena, a capo dell'omonima cosca, era in contatto diretto con Pietro Vernengo e Nitto Santapaola, boss della mafia siciliana.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans from Trapani and Mazara del Vallo allied with 'Ndrangheta clans Marando and Barbaro for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2007 l'operazione Stupor Mundi sgomina un traffico internazionale di droga gestito dai Marando e dai Barbaro di Platì che portavano la droga passante dal Belgio e dai Paesi Bassi in Piemonte e Lombardia in alleanza con le cosche del trapanese, di Mazara del Vallo.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans (Agate, Guttadauro) allied with 'Ndrangheta clans (Marando, Trimboli) for international drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003 con l'operazione Igres si scopre un'alleanza per il traffico internazionale di droga tra i Marando e i Trimboli di Platì e Volpiano, dall'altra i fratelli Agate di Mazara del Vallo e i Guttadauro di Bagheria.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Agate and Guttadauro allied with 'Ndrangheta clans Marando and Trimboli for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003 con l'operazione Igres si scopre un'alleanza per il traffico internazionale di droga tra i Marando e i Trimboli di Platì e Volpiano, dall'altra i fratelli Agate di Mazara del Vallo e i Guttadauro di Bagheria.

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia met to discuss heroin trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 1957, a meeting between Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia members was held at the Grand Hotel et des Palmes in Palermo to discuss the international illegal heroin trade in the French Connection.

descriptions: Both organizations were defendants in the same trial, indicating cooperation in the drug ring.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pizza Connection Trial (in full, United States v. Badalamenti et al. ) was a criminal trial against the Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia members acted on request of an American Cosa Nostra boss, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: the killing was organized by Bontade, Salvatore Greco 'Ciaschiteddu', and Giuseppe Di Cristina on the request of Angelo Bruno, a Sicilian-born Mafia boss from Philadelphia.

descriptions: The Sicilian Mafia and Italian-American Mafia are related, both using the name Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: the Sicilian Mafia and the related Italian-American mafia refer to their organizations as "Cosa nostra"

descriptions: The Sicilian Mafia and its American counterpart are studied together as related entities.
edges.evidence_quote: Academic studies of the original "Mafia", the Sicilian Mafia , as well as its American counterpart , generated an economic study of organized crime groups and exerted great influence on studies of the Russian bratva , the Indonesian preman , the Chinese triads , the Hong Kong triads, the Indian thuggee , and the Japanese yakuza .

descriptions: The Sicilian Mafia and its American counterpart are studied together as related organized crime groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Academic studies of the original "Mafia", the Sicilian Mafia , as well as its American counterpart , generated an economic study of organized crime groups and exerted great influence on studies of the Russian bratva , the Indonesian preman , the Chinese triads , the Hong Kong triads, the Indian thuggee , and the Japanese yakuza .

descriptions: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, created by Sicilian emigrants.
edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.

descriptions: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, formed by Sicilian emigrants.
edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.

descriptions: Turatello's gang cooperated with the Banda dei Marsigliesi in robberies and kidnappings.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang made billions of lire from these rackets, and also participated in several robberies and kidnappings, with the complicity of the Banda dei Marsigliesi, headed by Albert Bergamelli.

descriptions: Black Axe allied with the Porta Nuova clan of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Si scopre quindi un'alleanza tra il clan palermitano e l'organizzazione nigeriana.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra has had cooperative relationships with various Camorra clans in smuggling and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Vari clan di Camorra hanno intrattenuto rapporti, più o meno duraturi, con Cosa nostra, soprattutto nel campo del contrabbando di sigarette e del traffico di stupefacenti.

descriptions: Camorra was part of a collaboration with Russian mafia and Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2008 viene formalizzata la collaborazione fra mafia russa e Cosa nostra, 'ndrangheta e camorra.

descriptions: Cappello clan has rivaled Cosa Nostra for decades and now exerts influence over it in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: nella parte centrale ed orientale dell'isola prevale invece una struttura di tipo orizzontale, non più verticistica (« ad assetto variabile », l'ha definita un rapporto della DIA ), sul modello della Stidda e dei clan Cappello e Laudani , che ormai, dopo decenni di rivalità, esercitano un'enorme influenza su Cosa nostra, specialmente nel campo del traffico di stupefacenti

descriptions: Porta Nuova clan is part of Cosa Nostra and allied with Black Axe.
edges.evidence_quote: Si scopre quindi un'alleanza tra il clan palermitano e l'organizzazione nigeriana.

descriptions: Laudani clan has rivaled Cosa Nostra for decades and now exerts influence over it in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: nella parte centrale ed orientale dell'isola prevale invece una struttura di tipo orizzontale, non più verticistica (« ad assetto variabile », l'ha definita un rapporto della DIA ), sul modello della Stidda e dei clan Cappello e Laudani , che ormai, dopo decenni di rivalità, esercitano un'enorme influenza su Cosa nostra, specialmente nel campo del traffico di stupefacenti

descriptions: Turatello was a trusted friend of the Corleonesi leader.
edges.evidence_quote: He was also a trusted friend of Luciano Leggio, the head of the Corleonesi faction of the Sicilian Mafia.

descriptions: Gaetano Badalamenti had strong ties with the Detroit mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Gaetano Badalamenti, con solidi legami con la "famiglia" di Detroit

descriptions: Cosa Nostra supported E.V.I.S. with men and resources.
edges.evidence_quote: l'esercito clandestino del movimento indipendentista siciliano (M.I.S.) che, sostenuto da Cosa nostra e dagli agrari con uomini e mezzi, propugnava la separazione della Sicilia dal resto d'Italia

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Fidanzati and Emmanuello obtained drugs from 'Ndrangheta in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2009 i siciliani si rivolgono nuovamente alle 'ndrine per rifornirsi di droga, i Fidanzati di Palermo e gli Emmanuello di Gela prendevano la droga colombiana spedita attraverso i porti di Anversa e Amburgo.

descriptions: Santapaola-Ercolano is a family within Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: i gruppi criminali dei Nizza, Morabito e Saitta, concorrenti tra loro e tutti collegati alla storica "famiglia" Santapaola - Ercolano di Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Fidanzati and Emmanuello obtained drugs from 'Ndrangheta in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2009 i siciliani si rivolgono nuovamente alle 'ndrine per rifornirsi di droga, i Fidanzati di Palermo e gli Emmanuello di Gela prendevano la droga colombiana spedita attraverso i porti di Anversa e Amburgo.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra has links with the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: News articles also confirmed links between the Cosa Nostra and New York's Gambino crime family.

descriptions: The Grecos cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia in a drug shipment.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, mafia boss Cesare Manzella organized a drug shipment to the United States with the help of two Sicilian clans, the Grecos and the La Barberas.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Agate and Guttadauro allied with 'Ndrangheta clans Marando and Trimboli for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003 con l'operazione Igres si scopre un'alleanza per il traffico internazionale di droga tra i Marando e i Trimboli di Platì e Volpiano, dall'altra i fratelli Agate di Mazara del Vallo e i Guttadauro di Bagheria.

descriptions: The La Barberas cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia in a drug shipment.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, mafia boss Cesare Manzella organized a drug shipment to the United States with the help of two Sicilian clans, the Grecos and the La Barberas.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra supported the M.I.S. independence movement.
edges.evidence_quote: l'esercito clandestino del movimento indipendentista siciliano (M.I.S.) che, sostenuto da Cosa nostra e dagli agrari con uomini e mezzi, propugnava la separazione della Sicilia dal resto d'Italia

descriptions: Cosa Nostra's Camorra affiliates fought against Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: i camorristi campani furono ammessi in Cosa nostra e formarono delle succursali in Campania, che entrarono in competizione con la Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Raffaele Cutolo con un bilancio di centinaia di morti ammazzati nel periodo 1979-1984.

descriptions: Raffaele Cutolo opposed Cosa Nostra's activities in Campania, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra, difatti, era particolarmente invisa a Raffaele Cutolo, che si oppose alle attività dei mafiosi siciliani in Campania, da lui considerati alla stessa stregua di colonizzatori abusivi.

descriptions: Russian mafia formalized collaboration with Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2008 viene formalizzata la collaborazione fra mafia russa e Cosa nostra, 'ndrangheta e camorra.

descriptions: Stidda and the Cappello and Laudani clans have rivaled Cosa Nostra for decades and now exert influence over it in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: nella parte centrale ed orientale dell'isola prevale invece una struttura di tipo orizzontale, non più verticistica (« ad assetto variabile », l'ha definita un rapporto della DIA ), sul modello della Stidda e dei clan Cappello e Laudani , che ormai, dopo decenni di rivalità, esercitano un'enorme influenza su Cosa nostra, specialmente nel campo del traffico di stupefacenti

descriptions: Cosa Nostra fought against Stidda in a violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 1986 e il 1991, Cosa nostra si trovò a fronteggiare una nuova organizzazione criminale, la cosiddetta "Stidda"

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and Stidda agreed to a truce and peaceful coexistence.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra rischiò di soccombere nello scontro e nel 1991 si trovò costretta a stipulare un patto di "non belligeranza" con gli stiddari ribelli, che prevedeva una ripartizione dei proventi illeciti in parti uguali tra le due organizzazioni, che da allora hanno iniziato una pacifica convivenza durata fino ad oggi.

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia is listed as a business partner of Solntsevskaya Bratva in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Orekhovskaya gang (defunct) , Bulgarian mafia , Serbian mafia , Business partnerships with: , Colombian cartels , Chinese Triads , Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: The Çopja clan and Alibej clan were engaged in a violent rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: However, after a violent rivalry with the "Alibej clan" and extensive investigations, he was arrested in Dubai

descriptions: Maniac Murder Cult co-operated with Feuerkrieg Division.
edges.evidence_quote: Under him MKU co-operated with the Feuerkrieg Division.

Hammerskins.description: Neo-Nazi group formed in 1988 in Dallas, Texas, focused on producing and promoting white power rock music. It has chapters in the US, Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, and is described as the best-organized neo-Nazi skinhead group in the US. Members have been involved in violent attacks and hate crimes, though not centrally organized. The group maintains an active recruitment strategy and encourages military enlistment for a race war.

descriptions: Hammerskins were affiliated with 9% Productions.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hammerskins were affiliated with the record label 9% Productions.

descriptions: Finnish Atomwaffen members frequented Crew 38 headquarters.
edges.evidence_quote: Crew 38 came under police investigation in connection to the Kankaanpää terrorism arrests when the leader and members of the Finnish Atomwaffen group frequented the Crew 38 headquarters in Muurame and Kerava.

descriptions: Crew 38 (Hammerskins) cooperates with Blood & Honour in Finland.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2022 Finnish Blood & Honour has organized an annual "White Boy Summer Fest" together with Hammerskins and Veren Laki.

descriptions: Hammerskin Nation lists Blue-and-Black Movement as an affiliate.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: Blue-and-Black Movement

descriptions: Hammerskins emerged from Confederate Hammerskins.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hammerskins emerged in the late 1980s from the Dallas based Confederate Hammerskins.

descriptions: Lealtà e Azione is the Italian chapter of Hammerskins.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian chapter of the Hammerskins goes by the name "Lealtà e Azione".

descriptions: Hammerskin Nation lists Rise Above Movement as an affiliate.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: Rise Above Movement

descriptions: Crew 38 (Hammerskins) cooperates with Veren Laki.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2022 Finnish Blood & Honour has organized an annual "White Boy Summer Fest" together with Hammerskins and Veren Laki.

descriptions: Hammerskin Nation allowed KSS to help coordinate Hammerfest.
edges.evidence_quote: In late September 2003, Hammerskin Nation allowed KSS to help coordinate Hammerfest, which was held in Pennsylvania.

descriptions: Infobox lists Hammerskins as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Nations, Bandidos MC, Black Pistons MC, Breed MC, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Detroit Partnership, Grim Reapers MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Hammerskins, New Orleans crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Rock Machine MC

descriptions: The Outlaws are allied with the Hammerskins.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws are allied with the Bandidos, the Black Pistons, the Breed, the Grim Reapers, and the Gypsy Jokers motorcycle gangs, as well as the Aryan Nations and the Hammerskins

descriptions: Members of Volksfront were linked to Hammerskins.
edges.evidence_quote: A number of Volksfront's members were linked with the Hammerskins, a white supremacist group formed in Texas

descriptions: Mammoliti-Rugolo clan is closely linked to Mazzafero 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mammoliti-Rugolo clan is closely linked to the ... Mazzafero 'ndrina

descriptions: The Dog Posse challenged the Shower Posse for control of operations.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dog Posse challenged the Shower Posse for control of operations in New Jersey and Philadelphia.

descriptions: Tommy Banks (Minneapolis Syndicate) and Kid Cann (Minneapolis Combination) divided territories and cooperated.
edges.evidence_quote: Banks and Cann divided Minneapolis into territories with a handshake and worked side by side both during and after prohibition.

descriptions: Codispoti are allies of Nirta-Scalzone, with familial ties.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei Nirta-Scalzone (con cui hanno anche dei legami parentali)

descriptions: After 1995, the Giorgi allied with the Nirta Scalzone.
edges.evidence_quote: si alleano con i Nirta Scalzone

descriptions: Dorchester Ave Mavericks is listed as an ally of Columbia Point Dawgs in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dudley Street Park, Mission Hill, H-Block, Draper Street, Dorchester Ave Mavericks

Gran Grif.description: Gran Grif is the largest and most powerful criminal organization in the Artibonite department of Haiti, based in Savien. Founded around 2016 by former congressman Prophane Victor, it engages in murder, rape, mass abduction, forced child recruitment, and other violent abuses. The gang was responsible for 80% of reported civilian deaths in Artibonite in 2022 and perpetrated the 2024 Pont-Sondé attack that killed at least 115 people. It has been designated a terrorist organization by the Dominican Republic and the United States.

edges.target: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies
descriptions: Gran Grif is allied with G9 through the Viv Ansanm coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Viv Ansanm ( G-Pep and G9 )

descriptions: Gran Grif has a rival relationship with Ti Mepri.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ti Mepri

descriptions: Hells Angels targeted and killed members of the Dark Circle.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hells Angels would pay well for information identifying the members of the Dark Circle. Over the next two years, two members of the Dark Circle were murdered by the Angels

descriptions: Dark Circle member employed a hitman who also worked for Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: Gallant was employed by a member of the Dark Circle and also frequently carried out contracts for the Rock Machine

descriptions: Rock Machine formed the Alliance with Dark Circle to fight Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The local Rock Machine Motorcycle Club formed an affiliation, the Alliance, with Montreal crime families such as the Pelletier Clan, Dark Circle and other independent dealers who wished to resist the Hells Angels' attempts to establish a monopoly on street-level drug trade in the city.

descriptions: The Dark Circle was allied with the Rock Machine against the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The resulting investigation first alerted the public to the existence of the Dark Circle, and it was reported that the Hells Angels would pay well for information identifying the members of the Dark Circle.

descriptions: The Mazzara and Caterino-Ferriero clans were in conflict in Cesa.

Cárteles Unidos.description: Mexican criminal organization formed in 2010 to expel Los Zetas from Michoacán and Jalisco. Composed of members from various cartels including Tepalcatepec, Los Viagras, Sinaloa, La Familia Michoacana, Gulf, and Knights Templar. Known for drug trafficking in the Tierra Caliente region and ongoing conflict with CJNG. Designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department in February 2025.

descriptions: CJNG has prolonged confrontations with Cárteles Unidos, an alliance formed to contain CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: En el ámbito territorial, el CJNG ha mantenido enfrentamientos prolongados con diversos grupos, entre ellos La Familia Michoacana y Los Caballeros Templarios, así como con la alianza conocida como Cárteles Unidos, formada por múltiples células locales para contener su expansión.

descriptions: CJNG is in a violent fight against Cárteles Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: estados bajo asedio del CJNG y su lucha violenta en contra del CSRL (Cartel Santa Rosa de Lima) y Cárteles Unidos

descriptions: CJNG defeated La Resistencia in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: CJNG defeated La Resistencia and took control of Milenio's smuggling networks.

descriptions: Cárteles Unidos is listed as a rival of CJNG in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel , Tijuana Cartel , Santa Rosa de Lima Cartel , Los Viagras , Cárteles Unidos , Autodefensas , Nueva Plaza Cartel , Knights Templar Cartel , La Familia Michoacana , Cártel del Noreste , La Unión Tepito , Los Zetas , Zetas Vieja Escuela , Barrio Azteca (current status unknown) , Los Correa , Cartel del Abuelo , Grupo Sombra , Gente Nueva , La Nueva Familia Michoacana

Blood Family Mafia.description: Canadian street gang founded in 2006, active in Quebec, particularly in Quebec City, Côte-Nord, and Chaudière-Appalaches. Modelled after the Bloods and Central American maras, it engages in drug trafficking, torture, and murder. Known for conflict with the Hells Angels after refusing to pay a 10% tax. Current leader is David 'Pic' Turmel.

descriptions: BFM stopped paying tax to Hells Angels, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The BFM traditionally paid a 10% 'tax' to the Hells Angels, but in 2023 the group came into conflict with the Hells Angels after it ceased paying the 'tax'.

descriptions: Infobox lists Hells Angels as rivals of BFM.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels

descriptions: Hells Angels Quebec City chapter and Blood Family Mafia were in a turf war in 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2023, the Quebec City chapter of the Hells Angels was reported to be engaged in a turf war with the Blood Family Mafia.

descriptions: The Pecoraro-Renna clan is supported by the Mogavero family.
edges.evidence_quote: sostenute da altre famiglie criminali, come i Bisogni di Bellizzi ed i Mogavero di Pontecagnano Faiano

descriptions: Garuda Putih was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.
edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).

descriptions: Jorge Babu's group is historically known as a rival of Liga da Justiça.
edges.evidence_quote: Milícias historicamente conhecidas como rivais da Liga da Justiça teriam sido, segundo as investigações, o Comando Chico Bala, além do grupo comandado por Jorge Babu.

descriptions: Barillaro and Romano were allied against Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo in the Ciminà feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Ciminà : a partire dal 4 giugno 1966 ... vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra.

descriptions: The Women's Brigade is a sub-group of the Weather Underground.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Seattle Weather Collective , Women's Brigade

Azusa 13.description: Azusa 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in Azusa, California, founded in the 1960s. It is one of the most aggressive Sureño gangs, with around 400 active members. The gang is affiliated with the Mexican Mafia and engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and hate crimes, including a campaign of ethnic cleansing against African Americans. Many members have been indicted and convicted since 2011 for terrorizing African American residents.

descriptions: Duroc is listed as a rival of Azusa 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Duroc

descriptions: Azusa 13 is affiliated with the Mexican Mafia, as indicated by the '13' in its name.
edges.evidence_quote: the 13 in the name stands for the letter "M", indicating its affiliation with the Mexican Mafia

descriptions: Azusa 13 funnels money to the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang is known to tax drug sales in the area and funnels money to the Mexican Mafia

descriptions: Mexican Mafia gave a 'green light' to Azusa 13 and other gangs to attack African Americans.
edges.evidence_quote: the powerful Mexican Mafia, a prison-based gang, had given the "green light" to the many Latino gangs it controls in Southern California to terrorize and murder black people

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sentinel Boys 13

descriptions: Azusa 13 is listed as a rival of El Monte Flores.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bassett Grande and NS/ES Bolen, Lomas, Sangra, Azusa 13

descriptions: Azusa 13 is associated with the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Azusa 13 gang, associated with the Mexican Mafia, were indicted in 2011 for harassing and intimidating black people in Southern California.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Azusa 13

Nueva Plaza Cartel.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Jalisco, formed in 2018 as a split from the Jalisco New Generation Cartel. It operates primarily in the Guadalajara metropolitan area and disputes territory with CJNG. The group is involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, robbery, murder, and arms trafficking, and has received funding from the Sinaloa Cartel. Its founding leaders were Érick Valencia Salazar, Emilio Alejandro Pulido Saldaña, and Carlos Enrique Sánchez Martínez.

descriptions: Cártel Nueva Plaza was a splinter from CJNG, allied with Sinaloa against CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: creó el denominado Cártel Nueva Plaza y pactó una alianza con el Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: CJNG fights Nueva Plaza Cartel for control of Guadalajara.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting the Nueva Plaza Cartel for control of Guadalajara

descriptions: CJNG is in conflict with Nueva Plaza Cartel over Guadalajara.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting the Nueva Plaza Cartel for control of Guadalajara

descriptions: Nueva Plaza Cartel is at war with CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Valencia and Sánchez also began a war with their former cartel as well.

descriptions: CJNG fights Nueva Plaza Cartel for Guadalajara.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting the Nueva Plaza Cartel for control of Guadalajara

descriptions: Grupo Flechas provides weapons and funding to Cartel Nueva Plaza.
edges.evidence_quote: el Grupo Flechas le ha otorgado apoyo armamentistico y financiamiento a un grupo local denominado Cártel Nueva Plaza

descriptions: The two groups are in dispute over territory in the Guadalajara metropolitan area.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo se disputa los municipios de Zapopan, Tonalá, Tlajomulco de Zúñiga y Tlaquepaque, principales centros de operaciones de ambos grupos del narco

edges.evidence_quote: El Salto, Chapala, Juanacatlán, Poncitlán, Tlajomulco de Zuñiga and Ixtlahuacán de los Membrillos are the areas they dispute.

Dutch Mob.description: The Dutch Mob was a New York pickpocket gang active during the late nineteenth century. Formed in the late 1860s by Little Freddie, Mike Kurtz, and Johnny Irving, it grew to around 300 members. The gang operated in Manhattan's Bowery area, using staged street fights to pickpocket crowds. Police crackdowns in 1877 led to its dissolution.

descriptions: The Dutch Mob was formed by former members of the Italian Dave Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Formed during the late 1860s by Little Freddie, "Sheeny" Mike Kurtz , and Johnny Irving, former members of the Italian Dave Gang

descriptions: Black Cobra lists M16 as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Army , Black Scorpions , M16 , Black Ghost , Black Spider , Black Jackets

New Orleans crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in New Orleans, Louisiana, with a history dating back to the late 19th century. Engaged in racketeering, extortion, gambling, narcotics, and murder. Reached its height under Silvestro Carollo and Carlos Marcello. Declined after Marcello's imprisonment in the 1980s and death in 1993, though some elements may remain active.

descriptions: The New Orleans crime family is listed as an ally of the Chicago Outfit in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: New Orleans crime family

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: New Orleans crime family

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Chicago Outfit, Kansas City crime family, Los Angeles crime family, New Orleans crime family, St. Louis crime family

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chicago Outfit , Genovese crime family , Kansas City crime family , New Orleans crime family , Trafficante crime family

descriptions: Detroit boss Zerilli served alongside New Orleans boss Carlos Marcello on The Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: As a commission member Zerilli acted as a senior L.C.N. leader alongside the five New York City bosses and other top Mafia bosses from around the country at the time that included Stefano Magaddino of Buffalo, Sam Giancana of Chicago, Angelo Bruno of Philadelphia, Santo Trafficante Jr. of Tampa, Carlos Marcello of New Orleans and Raymond Patriarca Sr. of New England.

edges.evidence_quote: Al Capone supposedly traveled to the city demanding Carollo supply the Chicago Outfit (rather than Chicago's Sicilian Mafia boss Joe Aiello) with imported alcohol.

descriptions: New Orleans family sought assistance from Gambino family for video poker industry.
edges.evidence_quote: Carollo and an associate, Sebastian "Buster" Salvatore, met in New York with John Gotti and Salvatore "Sammy the Bull" Gravano of the Gambino family to seek assistance in taking over the soon-to-be-legalized video poker industry in Louisiana.

descriptions: Carlos Marcello began working for Frank Costello of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: This ordeal got him involved with Frank Costello, leader of the Genovese crime family in New York City, where he began working for Costello.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Genovese crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Buffalo crime family , Chicago Outfit , Cleveland crime family , Dallas crime family , Gambino crime family , Genovese crime family , Kansas City crime family , Los Angeles crime family , Philadelphia crime family , Trafficante crime family , Dixie Mafia , Outlaws MC

descriptions: Members of New Orleans and Genovese families were arrested together for video poker infiltration.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 1994, following an FBI sting dubbed "Operation Hard Crust", Carollo with 16 members of the Marcello, Gambino and Genovese families were arrested on charges of infiltrating the newly legalized Louisiana video poker industry.

descriptions: Phillip Kastel was an associate of both the New Orleans crime family (Marcello) and the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Kastel, a New York-born Jewish mobster, was an associate of the Marcello and Genovese families.

descriptions: The Matranga family was the predecessor of the New Orleans crime family; Carollo succeeded Matranga.
edges.evidence_quote: The Matranga crime family, established by Charles (1857 – October 28, 1943) and Antonio (Tony) Matranga (d. 1890), was one of the earliest recorded American Mafia crime families, operating in New Orleans during the late 19th century until the beginning of Prohibition in 1920.

descriptions: The New Orleans family used the Outlaws MC for debt collection and contract killing.
edges.evidence_quote: Under Marcello's rule, the family outsourced debt collection and contract killing to members of the Dixie Mafia and the Outlaws Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Outlaws MC as an ally.
